findings.title: Restore can report `restarted` without actually relaunching the surface process
findings.detail: Round-1 F1 is genuinely closed: the live-reconcile path is now wired through the production restore entry point, and focused live-path verification passed. But the rework introduced a B8 contract regression on the automatic-policy branch. ADR-0005 defines `restarted` as a new process launched under automatic policy. The current restore flow classifies surfaces first, and for an automatic-policy surface with a present executable/cwd it will produce `ClassRestarted`; then, in the commit path, it only installs the rebuilt replay/VT runtime and optionally stops an old live process. `RestoreSnapshot` never calls the PTY supervisor to spawn a replacement process anywhere on the restore path. That means restore can return `class: restarted` while no PTY was launched and the surface remains non-live. The new tests and CLI E2E do not cover automatic-policy restore at all: they cover live reconcile, manual-policy stopped, mismatch fail-closed, fresh-daemon never-live, and a separate explicit operator `restart` flow after restore. That violates the approved B8 acceptance contract and ADR-0005's meaning of `restarted`.

blocking_findings.title: Restore can report `restarted` without actually relaunching the surface process
blocking_findings.detail: The production restore path classifies automatic-policy surfaces as `restarted` via `persist.Classify`, but `RestoreSnapshot` never spawns a replacement PTY on restore, so the reported class can be false.
